Text:
Momentum continues.
 
Today we published our annual report for 2024/25, showing strong growth on both the top and bottom line. Revenue from continuing operations increased to DKK 516 million, while EBIT rose sharply to DKK 44 million – well ahead of our expectations for the year.
 
We delivered this result in a furniture market still marked by uncertainty. Over the year we gained market share, strengthened cash flow and reduced debt. In short, we are on the right course, and our strategic initiatives are having the desired effect.
 
We expect this positive momentum to continue in 2025/26, while maintaining a clear focus on sustainability – including our LOOP programme and our commitment to the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi).
 
Hear from our CEO, Anders Hedegaard Petersen, as he walks you through this year’s results and our outlook for 2025/26.

We look forward to continuing this journey together in the months ahead.

Text:
🌟 Workplace Burnout🌟

💡Have you felt so drained that when you want to finish a simple task it feels tiresome and hard. This is a sign of a burnout.

👉Burnout is a state of emotional, physical, and mental exhaustion caused by prolonged or excessive stress at work. Unlike ordinary fatigue, burnout builds up over time and can affect both performance and motivation.

Some common signs of burnout include :
💠Constant exhaustion, no matter how much you rest
💠Decreased productivity and difficulty concentrating
💠Feeling detached, unmotivated, or negative toward work
💠Physical symptoms like headaches, tension, or trouble sleeping

🤔Why is it important to talk about this? Because burnout doesn’t just affect individuals but also surroundings. 

✨ In workplace, when employees understand the signs of burnout, they can take proactive steps to protect themselves, and companies can create systems that encourage balance and care. This will help the employees to feel matters and the company culture can be healthier. 

#Pouchengroupindonesia #Manufacture #Atleticshoe #Burnout #Awareness #Workplace

Text:
Don't call our Formstrip a stripe 👟 

Ever wondered what that strip of material along the side of our footwear is? It's one of our most recognisable trademarks - and it isn't just for design. The Formstrip's story began in the mid-20th century, when football boots transitioned from thick materials to lighter ones. This shift brought a new challenge, as athletes struggled with stability during quick direction changes. Rudolf Dassler found a solution: a strip of leather on the side of the shoe to provide extra lateral support. The Formstrip made its debut at the 1958 World Cup in Sweden. Today, you can find it across all our silos - whether it's a KING football boot, Deviate NITRO™ running shoe or even the classic SUEDE.

See? It’s more than just a stripe 🐾

Text:
In 2005, Leah Taylor, VP, North America Retail - West, was a professional ballet dancer recovering from injuries and contemplating her next move, eager to find something she was equally as passionate about. With a Kinesiology degree and a love of movement, she took what she thought would be a temporary job at lululemon. “I loved the clothes I wore for dance and thought it would be a fun job.” She recalls.

What started as a part-time Educator position at the lululemon Robson St. store in Vancouver, evolved into a lifelong career. Leah discovered a deep passion for business and was inspired by the people around her. 

“I realized I have this passion for business and the culture. I was really impressed by the authenticity of the people I was around, how goal-oriented they were,” she says. “I thought, let me see what's here, how can I grow with this company?”

Leah's 20-year journey at lululemon is a story of trusting instinct, embracing challenge, and leading with impact. Read more below.

Text:
They told us it couldn’t be done. We did it anyway—boldly.

Back in the mid-2000s, when we first imagined a nylon that could be regenerated endlessly, many thought it was a dream too bold. Taking waste like fishing nets, industrial discards, and old carpets—and turning it into high-quality nylon? It sounded impossible.

But at Aquafil Group, we believed.

Not just in the technology.
We believed in a different way of doing business—one that takes what nobody wants and transforms it into something new and valuable.

I still remember the very first tonnes of ECONYL® nylon.
Few people knew what the #circulareconomy was.
Even fewer were willing to invest in it.

Today, I’m proud to share this milestone:
In the first quarter of 2025, ECONYL® brand nylon accounted for 60% of our total fiber revenues.

That means more than just a number.
It means 60% of what we sell in our fiber business is born from #waste, not fossil sources.
60% is driven by purpose, not compromise.
60% is proof that a different model is not only possible—it works.

Be bold. Make the impossible possible.
Resource_Use_reasoning: The post explicitly states that ECONYL nylon is made from waste inputs such as fishing nets, industrial discards, and old carpets instead of fossil-based raw materials, and highlights that 60% of fiber revenues now come from nylon “born from waste, not fossil sources.” This directly reflects reduced use of virgin materials and a more circular, resource-efficient supply chain.
Emissions_reasoning: There is no explicit mention of greenhouse gas emissions, carbon footprint, pollution, or emission reduction efforts associated with ECONYL or Aquafil’s operations.
Innovation_reasoning: The post describes imagining and developing “a nylon that could be regenerated endlessly” from waste and turning it into “high-quality nylon,” presenting ECONYL as a new technology and business model based on circularity. This is clear evidence of environmental technology and process/product innovation aimed at reducing environmental burdens through regeneration of materials.

Text:
Our Quick Qs series is a way to get to know our new and recently promoted leaders at Levi Strauss & Co. Meet Christian (Chris) Callieri, our new chief supply chain officer, through these quick questions. Welcome, Christian!  

1️⃣  What did you do before joining LS&Co.?  
I was working at Victoria's Secret as the chief supply chain officer, supporting teams across our Victoria's Secret, PINK and Beauty businesses.  
 
2️⃣  What attracted you to LS&Co.?  
There were many things that attracted me to LS&Co. First, I love the brand and products — I grew up wearing Levi's®, and the opportunity to be a part of this team was very exciting. Second, I have a passion for sustainability and admire the commitment LS&Co. has for the doing the right thing for the environment and all of our communities. These values were evident in everyone I met through the interview process. Finally, as I met with Michelle, I was energized by her desire to continue to build our supply chain to become a strong competitive differentiator for LS&Co. I’m excited to be a part of this incredible journey. 
 
3️⃣  Do you have a favorite personal story about LS&Co. or one of our products?  
I still remember being on a Boy Scouts camping trip as a kid, wearing shorts one evening as we were sitting around the fire. I started to get cold, so I went to change into my pair of Levi's® (which I think were 501® jeans). When I returned, my spot had been taken, so I sat on an empty log. To my surprise, it was a smoldering log — but I didn’t notice until I sat down. While my jeans got burnt, I did not. If I had been wearing any of the other pants I took with me I definitely would have had serious burns.  
 
4️⃣  What is the worst haircut you ever had? 
My mom used to give me a bowl cut when I was younger and I had to suffer a lot of teasing because of it. 
 
5️⃣  Who is the most famous person you’ve ever met? 
Sean Connery in St. Andrews, where we asked him to sponsor our golf team. 
 
6️⃣  Where was your favorite vacation? 
I love going to Africa for wildlife trips. My wife is Kenyan, and our latest trip to the Mara was my favorite as I got to enjoy it with our two girls.  
 
7️⃣  What’s one thing that no one would guess about you? 
For my undergraduate thesis I studied thermoregulation in flies! 
 
8️⃣  Are you a morning or night person? 
Absolutely a morning person.

Our products are built to last, to be loved and worn and loved again. That’s why we’ve launched the Levi’s® Wear Longer Project, a community initiative designed to equip young people with the skills and confidence to repair, refresh and reimagine their clothes — so they look better and last longer. 

Last year, we commissioned a survey and found that while Gen Z is leading the charge in thrifting, repurposing and self-expression, 41% of Gen Z reported having no repair or customization skills, like sewing a button or hemming a pair of jeans. The Wear Longer Project aims to change that. We’ve partnered with Discovery Education to create free, accessible resources to teach these basic skills in the classroom and beyond.

Text:
⌛ In a workplace, time is one of the most valuable resources. How we manage and use it can affect our productivity.

⏱️ Time efficiency is about working smarter by using every minute with focus and intention. In a large organization where collaboration, deadlines, and priorities often overlap, time efficiency allows us to achieve more with less pressure. It helps employees stay organized and balanced.

✨ So how can we use our time efficiently at work?
Here are a few effective habits that can help:
1. Set clear priorities.
Start your day by identifying the most important tasks that align with your goals or team objectives.
2. Plan your day ahead.
Use a planner or digital calendar to organize tasks and deadlines. 
3. Avoid multitasking.
While it may feel productive, multitasking often divides attention. Instead, concentrate on one task at a time.
4. Manage distractions.
Notifications, chats, and emails can break your focus easily. Allocate specific times to check messages or emails so you can stay in deep focus mode.
5. Take short breaks.
Efficiency doesn’t mean nonstop work. Short breaks help refresh your mind, improve focus, and maintain consistent performance throughout the day.
6. Reflect and adjust.
At the end of the day, take a few minutes to evaluate how you spent your time. Identify what worked well and what needs improvement.

💡 Using time efficiently will help you balance your productivity with well being. By managing time wisely, you can create space for creativity and continuous growth.

Text:
BIRKENSTOCK has historically been associated mainly with sandals.

But closed shoes are not an addition to our story. They are where it began. 

Long before the sandal became iconic, Carl Birkenstock developed the concept of the “Idealschuh”, a closed shoe built around the principle of anatomical support. That philosophy remains unchanged.

Today, we are steadily expanding our closed-shoe portfolio, from sneakers to boots and moc-toe silhouettes, strengthening our position as a year-round footwear brand. This is not a seasonal extension. It is a deliberate step toward greater resilience and diversification.

The numbers reflect this trajectory. In the first quarter, closed styles accounted for approximately 60 percent of revenue, reflecting the increasing balance of our portfolio across seasons.

At the core of this development is what has always defined us: anatomically engineered footbeds, carefully selected materials and functional design translated into contemporary silhouettes. With the launch of the HIGHWOOD MOC LACE LOW, a modern moc-toe silhouette building on the functional DNA of the HIGHWOOD boot, we continue to broaden our portfolio. 

Today, closed shoes are a structural part of our business and a key driver of a long-term, seasonally resilient growth strategy.

At #Dior, we empower our people to grow and be their best.
Meet Hasmik Minasyan and Antoine (Huasong) TAN, both VIP Client Managers at Christian Dior Couture and discover the behind-the-scenes of their #DreamInDior experience as they unveil the richness of their métiers in Paris, France.

When asked, ‘what does #DreamInDior mean for you and what amazes you the most in your work?’ Hasmik shares: ‘#DreamInDior means being part of a magical universe, experiencing every moment with the energy and power of the House of Dreams. It marks the beginning of every project I undertake, where each challenge becomes a catalyst for my professional growth - and that is what truly inspires me.’
 
Antoine (Huasong) adds: ‘#DreamInDior is the inspiration of the House through its history, heritage, legacy, culture and creativity that fuels my ambitions. It is about creating the Dream and then sharing them with our prestigious Clients. What I find most amazing in my role is the countless possibilities I have at my disposal to make our Clients’ experiences unforgettable and personalized.’

Text:
Our Quick Qs series is a way to get to know our new and recently promoted leaders at Levi Strauss & Co. Meet Anjana Perumal, our new VP of Global accounting, through these quick questions. Welcome, Anjana! 

1️⃣ What did you do before joining LS&Co.?  
Before this, I led global finance operations and strategy in global technology and retail companies. I have built and led high performing global teams across financial advisory services, finance operations, controllership and compliance. I have driven digital and finance transformation with a sharp focus on both performance and people.  
 
2️⃣ What attracted you to LS&Co.?  
The brand’s legacy and values — there is a grounded authenticity in the culture that resonates with me. I am a big admirer of brands that evolve with the times while staying true to their roots. 
 
3️⃣ What does success look like in your job?  
Success in Global Accounting means being a fully digital, high-performing, insight-driven organization. It is about having automated touchless processes, driving continuous improvements and offering insights that enable the business to move faster. We’ll know our team has made it when Global Accounting runs best-in-class practices and becomes a true partner to the business. 
 
4️⃣ What is your secret dream career?  
Running a beachside cocktail bar that hosts jazz nights and travel stories. 
 
5️⃣ What kind of pets do you have?  
I have a golden retriever named Zeus — who greets every guest like a VIP — and a beagle named Dude, the real boss of the house. 
 
6️⃣ Where was your favorite vacation?  
I recently visited Prague, “the Magical city.” You don’t just wander; you wonder with every step. 
 
7️⃣ What is your favorite quote? 
“When you want something, all the universe conspires in helping you achieve it.” — The Alchemist 
 
8️⃣ How do you unwind?  
Decoding teen-speak, being a part-time detective on crime shows and hanging out with Zeus and Dude — my unofficial therapist.

Text:
The strategic value of cross-industry expertise, shared competences and their role in driving innovation in space technology was at the heart of the panel hosted by Prada and Axiom Space during The Final Frontier – Venice, an event by Type One.

Together with Catie Hague VP of Communications Axiom Space, Russell Ralston, Executive Vice President of Axiom Space and Italian Astronaut Walter Villadei, #LorenzoBertelli shared how over twenty years of investment in R&D with Luna Rossa Team — racing for the America’s Cup — laid the groundwork for Prada’s leap into this exciting new chapter.

“Many brands talk about innovation. We practice and live it. We are likely the only company in our industry to have consistently pursued a non-fashion, high-performance R&D pathway like sailing. All these capabilities proved critical in the development of the AxEMU suit. We didn’t start from scratch, we leveraged a robust and distinctive technological foundation” Lorenzo Bertelli, Prada Group CMO & Head of CSR

Text:
Important notice to potential candidates: It has come to our attention that people may be posing as recruiters for Levi Strauss & Co. The LS&Co. talent acquisition team members and related agents thoroughly vet candidates through a screening and interview process. They will not ask you to provide personal financial or banking information.

All recruiting email correspondence from actual LS&Co. recruiters will come from individuals with a @levi.com email address. Any correspondence coming from an individual associated with LS&Co. using an email address other than one ending in @levi.com should be considered suspicious. We encourage you to report any concerns to us — or to validate the authenticity of a recruitment contact — by messaging us through the platform.  

Fraudulent activity, or if you've been the victim of a scam, should be reported as follows:

If you’ve received a scam message on LinkedIn: https://lnkd.in/gb-Qd_v
FTC Consumer Information: https://lnkd.in/dYUjMUrv
FBI Internet Crime Complaint Center: https://www.ic3.gov/

Text:
The path to success is full of turns – Mikaela Shiffrin inspires at every single one🏅⛷️ 

Mikaela Shiffrin started her career young and with an astonishing series of successes – including Olympic Gold Medals at the 2014 and 2018 winter games.  

Then came the devastating loss of her father in 2020; Beijing in 2022, which she left without wins; and a major injury in 2024.  

At times she considered stopping skiing altogether.  

But she confronted each loss by stepping into her boots, clicking back into her skis, and easing back into her side-to-side turns at breakneck speeds. 

When her mother asked what keeps her returning to her sport, she answered: 
“There’s this endless longing to do one more turn a little bit better each run.” 

That’s what she keeps doing. This time, this longing turned into her third gold medal, giving her a new title to add to her long list: more Alpine Skiing Olympic Gold Medals than any other American. 

To find out more about her winding path, watch this exclusive look at a view of her life only a mother could share: https://adidas.pub/45JJSKP

#MilanoCortina2026 #YouGotThis

Time to celebrate! We are proud that we’ve won the German Sustainability Award for Products in the “Resources” category for our Infinity +Green press felts ♻️ 

This innovation represents our commitment to transforming the paper industry in a sustainable way because:

✔️ Combining of Infinity +Green and press felt recycling reduces Scope 3 emissions by up to 31%. 📉 
✔️ Closed recycling loop reduces product carbon footprint by 16% 📉
✔️ 15% reduction in Scope 3 emissions with Infinity +Green 📉

As Anne Klaschka, Global Product Manager Press Felts, explains: 
“Infinity+Green opens up new opportunities for the paper industry to implement circular economy principles in practice and take responsibility for the future. Receiving the German Sustainability Award confirms that we are on the right track.” 

This success was only possible with a strong partner by our side. This is Aquafil Group for us: A recognized pioneer in polyamide 6 recycling. Together, we can push the boundaries of sustainable innovation and create real impact within the industry.

For Voith, sustainability is more than just a goal—it is firmly anchored in our corporate strategy. We take responsibility for the environment, society, and future generations by developing innovative technologies that conserve resources, reduce emissions, and promote the circular economy.
A heartfelt thank you to all employees, partners, and customers contributing to this great success. Together, we are shaping a sustainable future!

Text:
Two weeks to go until Clerkenwell Design Week, and if knowing that I'll be there to hang out and catch up wasn't enough, then I've got a really good reason why you should be there too.

Architizer Managing Editor and all-around superstar Hannah Rose Feniak will host four panel discussions throughout the week as part of the Design Dialogues programme, in partnership with our good friends at Design Milk. 

She'll be speaking with a fantastic line-up of voices from across our industry, with four sessions spanning material reinvention, brand identity, where we go next with biophilia and the route to certification. 

1. Future Nostalgia: Reinventing Materials
This session examines how circularity and adaptive reuse are influencing the next generation of material thinking.

Tuckey Design Studio (Catarina Kohut); Mamou-Mani Architects and Fab.Pub (Arthur Mamou-Mani); For Everyday Life (Colin McGadie).

2. True to You: Building a Design Brand with Integrity
In an era of constant exposure, this session dives into how firms define and protect their brand DNA beyond logos and visual identity.

Collective Architecture (Sophie Irvine); Ayo Abbas / Abbas Marketing;
Caro Communications (Anya Cooklin-Lofting).

3. Beyond Biophilia: The Next Phase of Nature-Integrated Design
With a focus on health outcomes and neuroarchitectural benefits, this talk looks at how nature-integrated design is evolving into a more rigorous and measurable practice.

SpaceInvader (Sarah Dabbs); KPF.

4. Circular pathways to certification: unlocking LEED, BREEAM, WELL and GENSLER with ECONYL® and R2R® (in partnership with Aquafil Group)
This panel explores how ECONYL® regenerated nylon and the R2R® program facilitate obtaining international sustainability certifications such as LEED, BREEAM, and WELL.

Cristina Stefani, Aquafil Group

If you want to find out more or meet up while the Architizer team are in town, just let me know!

#ClerkenwellDesignWeek #Architecture #Design #MaterialInnovation #Sustainability #CDW

Text:
Sustainability is not a side topic 

At Suominen, sustainability is a core element of our business model — guiding how we design products, run our manufacturing and create long-term value. 

Our vision is to be the frontrunner in nonwovens innovation and sustainability. 

Sustainability at Suominen begins with the recognition that our business model places us at the heart of several global environmental and social challenges. As a developer and manufacturer of nonwovens used largely in single-use applications, we operate in a value chain where raw material choices, manufacturing efficiency and product end-of-life all shape our impact. 

Through our double materiality assessment, we have identified our most significant impacts — including greenhouse gas emissions, resource use and the single-use nature of many end-products. 

These insights guide our Sustainability Agenda 2025–2030, focusing on innovation, operations and people. 

👉 Read more in our 2025 Annual Report, including our Sustainability Statement: https://brnw.ch/21x1plk

#sustainability #manufacturing #ESG

At Columbia Sportswear, doing the right thing for our communities isn’t just a value—it’s a commitment.
 
This past Thursday, our Community Impact team hosted a Partio (party on the patio - if you know, you know) at our Portland HQ campus, to encourage and inspire employees to get involved and give back to our communities.
 
We empower our global full-time employees to make a difference through:
🔹 16 hours of paid volunteer time annually
🔹 A donation matching program ($1000/match annually)
🔹 Long-standing partnerships with organizations focused on Conservation and creating Access to the Outdoors for Everyone like Leave No Trace, National Park Foundation, Access Fund and Planet Water.
 
From local parks to global initiatives, our teams are creating positive change where they live and work.🌎

Text:
This year at HUGO BOSS, we kept the momentum going well beyond #InternationalWomensDay.  

This week in Metzingen, we focused on how sharing knowledge and experience can help bridge the AI gap for women — led by women! Our campus became a hub for AI, learning, and empowerment: we kicked off our brand-new AI Learning Days format, tested skills in an interactive Prompt Arena, and offered hands‑on demos with VR headsets to explore the intersection of women’s health and AI. We were also excited to welcome renowned AI thought leader Nicole Büttner as a guest speaker for our Women in AI talk, sharing inspiring perspectives on how women are shaping the future of AI and innovation.  

When more women are part of the AI conversation, the solutions we create become smarter, fairer, and more inclusive. How do you create AI opportunities for women in your workplace? 

Learn more about International Women’s Day at HUGO BOSS: on.hugoboss.com/IWD26 

#IWD2026 #insideHUGOBOSS

Text:
Excited to see that sustainability in design continues to make steady progress in the U.S. And it’s interesting to see that it might not be quantified the way we originally thought. 

For example, according to the US Sustainable Design Report from Metropolis Magazine and Interface, “less than 3% of building square footage is formally certified under any sustainability standard—not because action isn’t happening, but because responsibility is spread across many frameworks.” 

At first glance, that statistic might make it seem like sustainability is stalled. But real progress isn’t something that can be reflected in a certification or a standard. It requires multiple systems, collaborators, and stakeholders across the value chain working together. 

That’s exactly what we see at Aquafil Group. With the ECONYL® brand process, we’ve transformed nylon 6 into a circular material by building new collection systems and partnering with like-minded organizations looking to push the needle on design and innovation. 

Sustainability is a team sport, and requires us to work together to make smarter decisions that drive meaningful change.

Read the full report: https://lnkd.in/e8ZvttkN

Text:
Plastic or Paper? The packaging decision many brands struggle with.

When it comes to garment packaging, the choice often comes down to two materials: LDPE plastic or paper. But which option actually performs best when you consider functionality, environmental impact, regulations, and circularity?
 
In our next Knowledge Hub webinar, we’ll explore some of the key factors brands consider when choosing packaging materials. Together with industry experts and partners, we’ll discuss:

✔ Functional, environmental and aesthetic differences between plastic and paper
✔ Regulatory considerations brands should be aware of
✔ Packaging innovations and solutions from Vela™ and WILDPLASTIC®
✔ A live panel discussion and Q&A on navigating packaging circularity
 
Meet the panel:
• Ester M., Commercial Director Europe, Vela™
• Marten Kronemeyer, Head of Sales, WILDPLASTIC®
• Christina Heines, Material & Innovation Specialist, Nilörn
• Jennifer Elze, Global Compliance Specialist, Nilörn
• Fredrik Clason, Group Sales & Marketing Manager, Nilörn
 
Date: 26 March 2026
Time: 15:00–15:45 CET
 
We’re opening this Knowledge Hub session to a wider audience, welcoming brands and industry professionals to join our next session. Reach out to knowledgehub@nilorn.com and we’ll share the registration details.
 
#Packaging #PackagingCircularity #KnowledgeHub #Webinar

Text:
We had the pleasure of being part of the opening event of the Euronext Sustainability Week 2025, “Driving Growth and Competitiveness through Sustainability”, on September 9 at Palazzo Mezzanotte in Milan.

•	99% of the leather purchased from LWG-certified tanneries  
•	Back to Earth Capsule Collection crafted from bio-based materials 
•	97% of direct suppliers in Italy, supporting local craftsmanship 
•	16.4% reduction of Scope 1-3 emissions in 2024 vs 2023 
•	Community initiatives in arts & culture, health, and women’s empowerment. 

We’re proud to continue this journey, shaping a luxury industry that draws inspiration from the past while always looking to the future.

Text:
🌱 Exciting news from XingFengMing Group!

We are proud to announce that XingFengMing and Leaf Bio have officially signed a strategic cooperation agreement, opening a new chapter of collaborative innovation in bio-based materials for the textile fiber industry.
Through this partnership, we aim to harness Leaf Bio’s cutting-edge FDCA and BioFleax™ technologies to explore high-performance, low-carbon solutions — from premium bio-based fibers to sustainable packaging. This step aligns with our commitment to “greener, differentiated, and innovation-driven” development and supports China’s dual carbon goals.

By combining our industry expertise with Leaf Bio’s pioneering research in furan-based materials, we hope to accelerate the green transformation of global textile fibers and contribute to a more sustainable future.

Text:
It’s been a busy time, but before any more time passes, I wanted to flag two recent happenings that say much about Levi Strauss & Co. as a company.  
  
One is what we did during Climate Week in New York City in late September. We started by launching a renewable electricity support initiative for suppliers in India, the Levi’s Energy Accelerator Program (LEAP), which is designed to bolster the sustainability aspirations of our suppliers in the country while also helping us further reduce our own emissions. Then we convened a pan-industry gathering of sustainability professionals in our New York office to share approaches on business and human resiliency in the face of extreme weather, a volatile external environment and a warming climate. To round it out, we staged a terrific panel moderated by Fast Company's Elizabeth Segran, Ph.D. that was focused on why people love, care for and repair clothing. The panel featured our Head of Global Design Innovation, Paul Dillinger; Yvonne Watson, the Vice Provost for Global Academic Strategies and Partnerships at the The New School; and Emma Rogue, thrifting influencer and founder of Rogue Garms. Their conversation explored the connection people form with clothing, why that connection deepens with time and care, and how we collectively can design (Paul), educate (Yvonne) and curate (Emma) to nurture those connections and enable a more conscious consumerism. You can see the full video here:  https://lnkd.in/gz2gA5Fx
  
The second event of note was our annual Seen & Heard employee conference, which, befitting our global footprint, was staged across our offices in Singapore, Brussels and San Francisco. Focusing on the theme of “cultural intelligence,” we talked about why being open, curious, diverse and inclusive – especially when serving fans and employees across the world – is so important to a business like ours. Employees heard from our leaders and fantastic external speakers like actress/creator/author Issa Rae, mountaineer David Lim, who led Singapore’s first team to summit Mt. Everest, and high performance psychologist and Red Bull base jumper Cedric Dumont. 
  
Our climate week efforts and Seen & Heard are each singularly important, but taken together, they demonstrate our sense of the right way to do business – an approach we call Profits through Principles. It’s an approach that has sustained this company for 170+ years and will continue to sustain us going forward.
Resource_Use_reasoning: The post describes a panel and broader initiative focused on why people love, care for, and repair clothing, and how to design, educate, and curate to nurture those connections and enable more conscious consumerism. This explicitly promotes repair, care, and extended product life, which directly relate to reducing material use and improving resource efficiency in the value chain.

Text:
🌤️ Sunny or Rainy — Which Weather Boosts Your Productivity? 🌧️

⭐ Productivity can be affected by many things and in this case, it's weather. Some people stay focused and energized under bright sunshine, while others feel more comfortable and calm when the rain is pouring outside. Both sunny and rainy days offer unique atmospheres that can influence how people work.

Here are a few common ways weather affects working habits:
1. Sunny Days ☀️
 The bright light often increases motivation and positivity. But the heat can also make workers feel tired or dehydrated faster. 
2. Rainy Days 🌧️
 The cool breeze helps some workers feel relaxed and focused. However, the cozy atmosphere can sometimes make people sleepy or less energetic.

💡 This topic speaks out how people can work differently based on their environment. Some perform best under the warm sunshine, while others prefer the peaceful rhythm of the rain.

Text:
⭐ Leadership starts with self-awareness and preparation. A leader carries broader responsibilities, from managing workflows and deadlines to guiding people with different roles and personalities. For you who are interested in becoming a leader, it is important to reflect on personal habits, attitudes, and ways of working first because your action can directly affect team morale, communication, and work results. 

👉 There are certain traits that a leader can bring and give negative impact to the team if they are not addressed early :
1️⃣ Poor emotional control, such as reacting with anger or frustration instead of clear communication
2️⃣ Procrastination, which can delay decisions and slow down team progress
3️⃣ Lack of accountability, including avoiding responsibility when mistakes happen
4️⃣ Poor listening habits, where team input is ignored or undervalued
5️⃣ Inconsistent work discipline, which can set an unhealthy example for the team
These behaviors can disrupt workflow, reduce trust, and create confusion.

🌏 Before aiming to lead others, take time to understand your strengths and work on areas that still need improvement. By preparing mentally, emotionally, and professionally, you as a future leader can create a more stable, respectful, and productive working environment for everyone.

#pouchengroupindonesia #manufacturingcompany #athleticshoe #careergrowth #leadership #habits

Samsonite Wins the Environment Gold Award 

Last week our Essens Circular suitcase won the prestigious Henry van de Velde Environment Gold Award 2026, recognising its strong approach to circular design and sustainable innovation.

Designed for durability, repair and reuse, this suitcase shows how circular principles can be applied at scale, from recycled materials to modular components and a digital product passport.

A great example of how design, innovation and responsibility can go hand in hand. Congratulations to everyone involved in bringing this project to life.

Text:
Take a closer look at the #TeamJapan official sportswear for the Milano-Cortina 2026 Olympic and Paralympic Winter Games.

The uniforms are designed around a concept that balances performance and sustainability.

The Podium Jacket (Outdoor) is made from waterproof and breathable materials, including GRS-certified recycled down. An internal baffle structure strategically positions heat-retaining materials to help minimize interference with movement.

The Podium Jackets and Pants (Outdoor and Indoor) feature zipper pulls that repurpose waste material from our suppliers and a carbon footprint for the entire product lifecycle. Structures and individual parts are designed for ease of use in winter conditions, including by wheelchair athletes.

The Shoe is based on the GEL-Trabuco MT GTX, a trail running shoe designed for comfort and durability. The tongue includes approximately 5% recycled polyester from end-of-life pairs of the NIMBUS MIRAI circular running shoe released by ASICS in 2024.

ASICS is proud to be a TEAM JAPAN Gold Partner (Sporting Goods).

アシックスのデザインによる、ミラノ・コルティナ2026オリンピック・パラリンピック冬季競技大会のTEAM JAPANオフィシャルスポーツウェア。その機能やこだわりをご紹介します。

TEAM JAPANが着用するオフィシャルスポーツウェアは、パフォーマンスとサステナビリティの両立をコンセプトに設計されています。

ポディウムジャケット（アウトドア）には、防水性・透湿性に優れた素材を使用し、GRS認証のリサイクルダウンを採用。体の動きを妨げにくいよう、保温素材を戦略的に配置する内部構造を取り入れています。

ポディウムジャケットおよびパンツ（アウトドア・インドア）には、当社が提携しているサプライヤーから出た廃材を使用したジッパーの引き手パーツを採用し、製品ライフサイクル全体におけるカーボンフットプリントを算定。車いすアスリートを含め、冬季環境下でも使いやすい構造やパーツ設計を施しています。

シューズは、快適性と耐久性を追求したトレイルランニングシューズ「GEL-Trabuco MT GTX」をベースに開発。シュータンの裏材の約5％に、2024年にASICSが発表した循環型ランニングシューズ「NIMBUS MIRAI」の使用済みシューズから再生されたリサイクルポリエステルを採用しています。

アシックスはTEAM JAPANゴールドパートナー（スポーツ用品）です。

Text:
We are proud to announce that our Quality Control Laboratory at Five Star Enterprises has achieved Shoe and Allied Trades Research Association (SATRA) Gold Status Certification 

SATRA certification is an independent, globally recognized mark of quality, safety, and performance for footwear, confirming that products meet rigorous industry standards.

Five Star’s achievement of SATRA Gold Certification marks the first company in the entire Western Hemisphere to attain this prestigious certification. Of the 269 SATRA Certified Laboratories worldwide only five others have Gold Status.

Text:
Fishing nets must withstand harsh marine conditions and be replaced often to meet strict food safety standards.

They’re created to last and rarely with a second life in mind.

So how can nets be designed not just for durability, but also for circularity?

That’s the bold question Diopas S.A. asked when they began developing the first circular fishing net made from recycled materials. There was no blueprint, just a big idea and the right partners to make it real.

“The idea of creating the first circular fishing net was challenging at first because there was no example we could work from,” shared Magdalini Diamantidou, Marketing & Project Coordinator at Diopas. “But Aquafil Group made the process easier by providing an excellent quality of recycled nylon yarn.”

Made from ECONYL® Regenerated Nylon and now in real-world testing at Philosofish’s Ag. Serafeim site, this new net represents more than product innovation; it’s a circular shift in how gear is made, used, and recovered in aquaculture.

Read the full story in the comments below.

Text:
We did it! This week ASICS received the Grand Prize at the Corporate Governance of the Year 2025 awards held in Tokyo.

The award recognizes our clear management policy and our commitment to stakeholder dialogue. This includes selling all strategic holdings, making disclosures, and sincerely engaging in dialogue with investors. We were also commended for pursuing business transformation while linking this to executive pay and other forms of profit-sharing. 

It was an honor to join other ASICS colleagues to collect the Grand Prize from the distinguished judges including Mr. Toyama, Chairman of the Japan Association of Corporate Directors.

During the ceremony, Chairman Toyama surprised us by pointing to the ASICS RUNWALK business shoes he has been wearing for many years. Another judge, Professor Ito of Hitotsubashi University, commented on the exceptional comfort of his GEL-NIMBUS running shoes.

We are delighted to get the thumbs-up for corporate governance as well as for our shoes!

#InsideASICS

Text:
Specialized Racing Teams Up with Samsonite for a Seamless Travel Experience.
As of this year, Specialized Racing Team riders and staff will embark on their global mountain biking adventures with the trusted support of Samsonite.

For a professional mountain biking team, the journey is just as important as the race itself. From the rugged landscapes of South America to the charming trails of Europe and high-altitude circuits in Asia, Specialized Racing Team covers thousands of kilometers each season. With Samsonite as a new partner, the team will travel in unparalleled style and comfort, equipped with the brand's premium luggage and backpacks designed for both durability and sustainability.

In addition to our Proxis luggage, team riders and staff will also rely on Samsonite’s Ecodiver backpacks for hassle-free day-to-day travel.

Scott Jackson, Leader - Sports Marketing at Specialized, shared his enthusiasm for the partnership: “Being an elite sports team always on the go, we require luggage that can endure the hundred plus days we travel each year. This is why teaming up with Samsonite is a natural fit for us. Their cutting-edge luggage is crafted to tackle the demands of global travel, and we’re excited to partner with a brand that prioritizes quality and innovation. Starting in 2025, we look forward to sharing our journey with Samsonite.”

Discover the collections here: https://lnkd.in/eF5-iZAr

Text:
Aquafil has been awarded the EcoVadis Platinum Medal, one of the highest recognitions from the leading international platform that evaluates companies on their environmental and social responsibility performance.

The Platinum level is reserved for the top 1% of more than 150,000 companies assessed globally. It is assigned to organizations that complete the full evaluation and demonstrate a solid management system aligned with the EcoVadis methodology across four key areas: environment, labor and human rights, ethics, and responsible procurement.

This achievement reflects our ongoing dedication to integrating responsible practices into daily operations and across the entire value chain.
It also highlights a long-term approach to corporate responsibility and the progress made over the years.
It’s a result we can be proud of, and it encourages us to continue strengthening this journey together, always looking ahead and improving step by step.

🔗 Want to learn more about our EcoVadis Platinum result? Link in the comments.

Text:
#youngone #bangladesh

'Beyond the Stereotype' - this fascinating, in-depth report looks at how the readymade garment industry has quietly transformed over the past decade.

Here is an excerpt, the full report can be accessed via the link below.

'Beyond the Stereotype: Rethinking Bangladesh’s Textile Industry exposes a critical gap between perception of Bangladesh versus reality. Today, Bangladesh stands as one of the most advanced manufacturing hubs in the global apparel industry. It leads the world in green-certified garment factories. 

It has reformed its labor laws, built a robust regulatory infrastructure, and pioneered circular fashion systems at scale. Over 4.5 million workers—most of them women—are employed in an industry that has become central not only to the country’s economy, but to the global supply chain, with textile exports reaching over 150 countries. 

Factory safety systems have been overhauled, union representation has expanded fivefold, and environmental standards have become embedded in national policy. In key areas, Bangladesh is setting—not following—the global standard. 

Yet, global media narratives have not kept pace. In much of the Western press, Bangladesh remains anchored in 2013: a country defined by tragedy and ethical risk. A decade after the Rana Plaza collapse triggered international outrage, the story has barely moved forward. Progress is treated as provisional. Innovation is ignored. Reform is either downplayed or doubted. In this report, Taiwo Meghoma, Uchenna Ekwo, and Nathalie Beasnael set out to correct that record to explain why it matters

Text:
A month of movement, strength, and sisterhood: With Women We Run 🏃‍♀️💜 

With Women We Run (WWWR) is our women-led running movement, with active communities in 47 countries across 5 continents. Together, we’ve created a truly global celebration of community and female empowerment by amplifying women’s voices in running. 🌎✨ 

Here’s how we showed up to make November unforgettable:  

🫶 Creating spaces: Adidas Runners Doha hosted a series of classes to bring more women into sport. From Pilates to sound healing, all activations were designed to create a safe and inclusive space for women in Doha, culminating in a women’s-only run festival.  

💪 Expanding spaces: Over 20 European communities competed in a backyard ultra marathon at Sports Base Berlin. More than a race, it was a celebration of women’s endurance and strength. 
 
✊ Protecting spaces: Bogotá, Buenos Aires and Mexico City united to mark the International Day for the Elimination of Violence Against Women, turning invisible harassment into a visible act of resistance with 5K runs, with a minute of silence at the heart.  

🏃‍♀️ We bookended the month with an unforgettable marathon series – starting with NYC Marathon and closing in Beijing and Shanghai – spotlighting incredible community runners chasing PBs.  

Here’s to every mile, every win, and every woman! 🙌 

#adidasRunners #WithWomenWeRun

Text:
Not every corporate sustainability action will grab headlines, nor should they. The landscape has changed considerably in recent years -- for the better, I'd argue. There’s more rigor, more skepticism, much better data and a sharper focus on results than there was five or ten years ago. It’s become clear that real progress requires committed, consistent work that prioritizes impact, addresses the real-world implications of our operations and aligns with long-term business strategies. 
 
And while the quieter, unflashy moves that occur “behind the scenes” might not be making major headlines, they're just as critical as the big ones. They help us stay focused on our goals, execute against our plans, and deliver results over time.

With that in mind, I thought I’d use this space to highlight some of the more subtle initiatives we’re driving forward at Levi Strauss & Co., alongside our bigger swings. When added together, they demonstrate how we’re embedding sustainability across our operations, learning and adapting along the way as we work to drive progress across our three main sustainability pillars of climate, consumption, and community.

For now, I’ll call it #sustainabilityspotlight (but I’m open to suggestions about the name).
 
For example: many suppliers want to make their operations more sustainable but don’t have access to the capital they need to make changes in their infrastructure and processes. So, we’re collaborating with the IFC - International Finance Corporation and their Global Trade Supplier Finance program to address the financing gap, helping suppliers that perform well on our Supplier Code of Conduct find the funding to strengthen foundational compliance measures – such as employee education on labor rights and chemical labelling.  Over the years, we’ve refined our approach with IFC to not only improve compliance but also unlock broader sustainability initiatives, including renewable energy adoption.

Through this program, Epic Group—one of our key suppliers—has accessed more favorable financing by steadily improving their Code of Conduct scores and then used that funding to drive improvements in water use, energy efficiency and waste reduction at the factory level. Financing solutions like this are not one-size-fits-all, so it’s important we, as sustainability practitioners, take note of what’s working and how we can leverage those learnings elsewhere.

Read more about how the program supports suppliers here: https://lnkd.in/gNhNqPCA 

And check back soon for my next #sustainabilityspotlight on the work we’re driving forward at LS&Co..

"Growth brings responsibility. But even more, it brings opportunity. Our aim is to prove that performance, design and impact can move together in every stride – cutting our footprint, improving working conditions across our value chain and contributing to progress where we operate while engineering the gear that inspires people to move,” Begüm Kürkçü, Senior Director - Global Sustainability at On.

Introducing our 2025 Impact Progress Report – a candid look at our journey, reflecting on 15 years of On – sharing areas of progress while acknowledging our toughest challenges: minimizing our environmental impact in harmony with rapid growth, scaling circularity, and advancing living wages and social conditions in our supply chain to strive toward leaving a better footprint behind.

Presented in two-parts, inside you’ll find human stories behind our strategy, showcasing the people behind how decisions are made in the day-to-day. And, in part-two, discover the data and disclosures that hold us accountable.

Text:
Christian Frigast to depart Pandora Board of Directors. 

Deputy Chair, Christian Frigast has informed the Board that he will not stand for re-election at the next Annual General Meeting in 2026.
 
Christian Frigast has played an instrumental part in where Pandora is today. He was Managing Partner at the private equity firm Axcel, which acquired Pandora from the company’s founders in 2008 and led the company to its IPO in 2010. Since then, Christian Frigast has served as Deputy Chair on Pandora’s Board. 
 
“Christian was a key figure in Axcel’s investment in Pandora in 2008, and he has been instrumental in Pandora’s journey to become the world’s largest jewellery brand. On behalf of Pandora and the Board, I would like to express my gratitude to Christian for his dedicated service,” says Peter Ruzicka, Chair of Pandora’s Board of Directors. 
 
Christian will serve as Deputy Chair until the next Annual General Meeting in 2026. 
 
Read more here: https://lnkd.in/dB4EF-xS

Text:
Earlier today, we published our Annual Report for 2025. Despite ongoing macroeconomic headwinds, we delivered on our financial targets and laid a solid foundation for the execution of our CLAIM 5 TOUCHDOWN strategy through 2028. Supported by a robust performance in the fourth quarter, we achieved solid top- and bottom-line improvements in 2025. 
 
A few key highlights: 
 
⚫ Currency-adjusted Group sales up 2% to EUR 4.3 billion   
⚫ EBIT up 8% to EUR 391 million, with EBIT margin up 80 basis points to 9.2% 
⚫ Prioritizing total shareholder return via a EUR 200 million share buyback through 2027 and an adjusted dividend proposal   
 
This past year, we reassessed our strategic direction and successfully shaped our company for the next chapter. With the launch of CLAIM 5 TOUCHDOWN, HUGO BOSS introduced a clear framework to further drive excellence in our brands, distribution, and operations, and set the course for sustainable, profitable growth.  
 
The motto of our newly released Annual Report is “SHAPE,” as we continue to shape the future of HUGO BOSS. Learn more: on.hugoboss.com/AR25

Text:
Today, we can share that Berta de Pablos-Barbier will take over as our new CEO on 1 January. The transition, initially set to complete at the Annual General Meeting in March, will be finalised earlier than planned, following a seamless handover from our current CEO Alexander Lacik. 
 
Alexander will remain with Pandora as Special Advisor to the Board and ELT until the AGM on 11 March 2026. 
 
At the same time, we are also happy to announce the appointment of Jennie Farmer as Pandora’s new CMO. Jennie is currently our SVP, Brand Experience & Channels, and will take on the new position from January 1.  
 
Peter Ruzicka, Chair of Pandora, commented: 
“The handover from Alexander to Berta has been exceptionally smooth. With Jennie Farmer stepping in as CMO, we are pleased to be able to conduct the leadership transition faster than planned. We look forward to the next era of growth under Berta’s leadership.” 
 
Read the full story here: https://lnkd.in/eZSGu7fx 
 
#Leadership #Pandora #CEO #CMO

Text:
Today we announce that our CEO Alexander Lacik has decided to retire at the next annual general meeting on 11 March 2026 after almost seven years as President and CEO of Pandora. He will hand over to Chief Marketing Officer (CMO) Berta de Pablos-Barbier who will become the new President and CEO. She will lead our continued strategic evolution as a full jewellery brand, building on the strong results during Alexander Lacik’s tenure.  
 
Peter Ruzicka, Chair of the Board of Directors says:  
“Berta is a visionary leader with great analytical skills and a perfect mix of experience from top brands across luxury, fashion and fast-moving consumer goods. She is the right person to lead our continued growth, and I am pleased that we can maintain strategic focus and speed as we make a smooth and orderly leadership transition. Alexander leaves a strong foundation for future growth. We will thank him in due course for his tremendous achievements and for transforming Pandora to a completely different company than the one he joined in 2019.” 
 
Read more in the announcement here: https://lnkd.in/d4uzbrTE

Text:
We’re proud to share that on March 13, we opened our first store in Warsaw, Poland, marking our 100th store in EMEA. What started as a single location in London’s Covent Garden in 2014 has grown into a footprint spanning 20 markets across the EMEA region through a mix of company-operated and franchise partnerships, made possible by the passion, commitment, and belief of our teams.  

Congratulations to all of those who played a part in this huge achievement—we can’t wait to welcome our guests in.
